title: Andaman Islands 7-Day Beach Getaway
metaTitle: Andaman Islands 7-Day Beach Getaway | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 7-day trip to Andaman Islands, India: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Cellular Jail National Memorial and Radhanagar Beach, Havelock (Swaraj Dweep). Prices from $1824 per person (estimate).
highlights: Scuba diving off Havelock and Neil Island
overview: Spend 7 days discovering Andaman Islands, India — Tropical maritime climate: warm and humid year-round (highs 28-31°C), with a monsoon split across two seasons (southwest monsoon June-September, weaker northeast monsoon November-December) that can disrupt inter-island ferries. November to April for calm seas, clear skies and the best diving/snorkeling visibility (highs 28-31°C); the May-September monsoon and cyclone season brings rough seas and disrupted ferry schedules. This itinerary blends Cellular Jail National Memorial, Radhanagar Beach, Havelock (Swaraj Dweep), Corbyn's Cove Beach with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
6 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Cellular Jail National Memorial, Radhanagar Beach, Havelock (Swaraj Dweep), Corbyn's Cove Beach
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Andaman Islands
itinerary.description: Land at Veer Savarkar International Airport (Port Blair) (IXZ) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Cellular Jail National Memorial — Colonial-era prison for freedom fighters, with an evening light-and-sound show. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Andaman Islands: Radhanagar Beach, Havelock (Swaraj Dweep) & Mahatma Gandhi Marine National Park boat & snorkeling trip
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Radhanagar Beach, Havelock (Swaraj Dweep) — Award-winning white-sand beach, activities and beach shacks ticketed separately. In the afternoon, continue to Mahatma Gandhi Marine National Park boat & snorkeling trip — Coral reef snorkeling and glass-bottom boat tour off Wandoor. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: Mahatma Gandhi Marine National Park boat & snorkeling trip

itinerary.title: Andaman Islands: Scuba diving, Havelock/Neil Island & North Bay Island glass-bottom boat & snorkeling
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Scuba diving, Havelock/Neil Island — Reef and wreck diving with PADI-certified operators. In the afternoon, continue to North Bay Island glass-bottom boat & snorkeling — Coral viewing near Port Blair, popular half-day trip. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: North Bay Island glass-bottom boat & snorkeling

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Havelock Island (Swaraj Dweep)
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Havelock Island (Swaraj Dweep), a popular side trip from Andaman Islands. Return to Andaman Islands in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Havelock Island (Swaraj Dweep)

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Neil Island (Shaheed Dweep) natural bridge — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Andaman Islands: Ross Island (Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose Dweep) & Limestone Caves, Baratang Island
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Ross Island (Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose Dweep) — Former British administrative headquarters, now ruins reclaimed by nature. In the afternoon, continue to Limestone Caves, Baratang Island — Boat and jungle-trek excursion to natural limestone caves. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: Ross Island (Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose Dweep)

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Elephant Beach snorkeling, Havelock or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to IXZ for your departure flight.

travelTips: Book inter-island ferries to Havelock and Neil Island several days in advance during the November-April peak season.
Foreign nationals should keep their Restricted Area Permit (issued at Port Blair airport) handy — it may be checked at hotels and ferry counters.
Elephant Beach and North Bay have the most accessible snorkeling; serious divers should base in Havelock for the best dive sites.
Radhanagar Beach is best visited in late afternoon for sunset, but arrive with enough daylight left to return before dark.
Cellular Jail's light-and-sound show in the evening adds significant historical context beyond the daytime visit.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Reef-safe sunscreen to protect the coral
Water shoes for rocky entry points at some beaches
A dry bag for camera/phone protection on boat transfers

thingsToAvoid: Any attempt to visit or photograph the Jarawa or Sentinelese tribal reserves — this is illegal and dangerous.
Touching or standing on coral while snorkeling — it is fragile and slow to recover.
Traveling during cyclone warnings (typically Oct-Dec transition) — ferries and flights can be cancelled with little notice.
Booking only one full day for Havelock — most highlights need at least two nights to enjoy without rushing.
Unlicensed dive operators — verify PADI/SSI certification before booking scuba trips.
topRestaurants: Full Moon Cafe, Havelock — Seafood & Continental, beachfront ($$)
Anju Coco Resort restaurant, Havelock — Multi-cuisine, beachfront ($$)
Icy Spicy Restaurant, Port Blair — South Indian & Seafood ($)
Excellent Seafood, Port Blair — Andaman Seafood ($$)
nightlife: Beachfront bonfire evenings on Havelock/Neil Island
Stargazing on quieter beaches away from Port Blair

hiddenGems: Ross Island ruins overtaken by roots and deer
Baratang limestone caves and mud volcanoes
familyActivities: Glass-bottom boat ride at North Bay Island
adventureActivities: Scuba diving off Havelock and Neil Island

luxuryExperiences: Overwater-style luxury villa stay at a Havelock beach resort

PADI advanced dive course with a private instructor
honeymoonExperiences: Private candlelight dinner on the sand
Couple's snorkeling trip to a quiet reef
Overwater villa stay at a Havelock resort
budgetExperiences: Public ferry (rather than private) between Port Blair and Havelock
Free beach time at Corbyn's Cove and Kalapathar
Self-guided snorkeling gear rental instead of a guided tour
localEtiquette: Respect designated no-swim and coral-protection zones at marine national parks such as Mahatma Gandhi Marine National Park.
Never touch or stand on coral while snorkeling or diving — reef damage here is taken seriously.
Photography inside Cellular Jail's solitary cells is welcomed, but stay quiet and respectful — it is a site of real historical suffering.
Indigenous tribal reserves (Jarawa, Sentinelese) are strictly off-limits — do not attempt to photograph, contact or travel toward tribal reserve areas.
Modest swimwear is appreciated at public beaches shared with local families, versus more remote resort beaches.
localTransportGuide: Government and private ferries connect Port Blair to Havelock and Neil Island (book Makruzz or Green Ocean ferries ahead in peak season); within Port Blair, autos and taxis are the norm, while Havelock and Neil are best explored by rented scooter. Inter-island travel by air is limited, so most movement is by sea.
bestTimeToVisit: November to April for calm seas, clear skies and the best diving/snorkeling visibility (highs 28-31°C); the May-September monsoon and cyclone season brings rough seas and disrupted ferry schedules.
weather: Tropical maritime climate: warm and humid year-round (highs 28-31°C), with a monsoon split across two seasons (southwest monsoon June-September, weaker northeast monsoon November-December) that can disrupt inter-island ferries.
visaInformation: Most foreign nationals require an Indian e-Visa applied for online before travel; foreign nationals also need a free Restricted Area Permit (RAP) for the Andamans, typically issued automatically on arrival at Port Blair airport alongside immigration. Indian citizens need no visa or permit; OCI/PIO cardholders travel visa-free with a linked foreign passport.
averageFlightTime: ~10.1h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Andaman Islands?
faqs.answer: Most foreign nationals require an Indian e-Visa applied for online before travel; foreign nationals also need a free Restricted Area Permit (RAP) for the Andamans, typically issued automatically on arrival at Port Blair airport alongside immigration. Indian citizens need no visa or permit; OCI/PIO cardholders travel visa-free with a linked foreign passport.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Andaman Islands?
faqs.answer: November to April for calm seas, clear skies and the best diving/snorkeling visibility (highs 28-31°C); the May-September monsoon and cyclone season brings rough seas and disrupted ferry schedules. Tropical maritime climate: warm and humid year-round (highs 28-31°C), with a monsoon split across two seasons (southwest monsoon June-September, weaker northeast monsoon November-December) that can disrupt inter-island ferries.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Andaman Islands safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Andaman Islands is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Andaman Islands?
faqs.answer: The local currency is INR. Carry some cash for smaller vendors — card acceptance can be inconsistent outside major hotels and malls.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: Yes — Andaman Islands is one of our most-booked honeymoon destinations, especially the Sunset at Radhanagar Beach together experience. It also works well for families — highlights include Glass-bottom boat ride at North Bay Island.

reviews.author: Arjun Botha
reviews.title: Unforgettable trip!
reviews.body: Booked this as a solo trip and it was well paced. Elephant Beach snorkeling, Havelock in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Priya Cohen
reviews.title: Loved every moment
reviews.body: Booked this as a couple trip and it was well paced. Kalapathar Beach, Havelock in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Priya Patel
reviews.title: Exceeded our expectations
reviews.body: Booked this as a family of 4 trip and it was well paced. North Bay Island glass-bottom boat & snorkeling in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Noah Tanaka
reviews.title: Best vacation we've had
reviews.body: Our trip to Andaman Islands was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Samudrika Naval Marine Museum grounds was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Lukas Novak
reviews.title: Would book again in a heartbeat
reviews.body: Andaman Islands is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Limestone Caves, Baratang Island. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Lukas Johnson

reviews.body: Andaman Islands is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Limestone Caves, Baratang Island. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Limestone Caves, Baratang Island and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.
